entry.title: SAC and Joule
entry.content: Can I call a Joule Agent From SAC to trigger an action in S4. What would be required to set it up ?

entry.title: Error while creating Tunnel connection in SAC

While creating a Tunnel connection between SAC and datasphere we are getting a error please find attachment for error screenshot.

entry.title: [SAC Trial] Authorization Request Error when connecting to SAP Datasphere (OData Connection)
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently trying to create an OData connection from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC Trial – AP11) to SAP Datasphere.

However, I am facing an issue during the OAuth authentication step.

Environment:

  • SAC URL: https://academy-t-sac.ap11.hcs.clod.sap
  • Datasphere URL: https://academydatasphere-1.ap11.hcs.cloud.sap

Connection Configuration:

  • Authentication Type: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code
  • Data Service URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.ap11.hcs.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/SPACE_00005323/Analytic_Model_GE298598
  • Token URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.authentication.ap11.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
  • Authorization URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.authentication.ap11.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize
  • OAuth Client ID: (from SAP Learning guide)
  • Secret: (from SAP Learning guide)

Issue:
When I click "Create", SAC redirects to the authorization URL but shows the following error:

What I have tried:

  • Switched endpoints from academydatasphere-1 to academy
  • Verified Space ID (SPACE_00005323)
  • Checked Data Service URL format
  • Tested authorization endpoint directly in browser

Questions:

  1. Are the academydatasphere-1 endpoints still valid for AP11 trial?
  2. Has the OAuth client (from SAP Learning material) expired or changed?
  3. Is there any required redirect URI configuration for SAC trial connections?
  4. What is the correct configuration for current SAP Datasphere + SAC trial landscape?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: How to delete all private bookmarks in SAC Planning Stories as an admin
entry.content: We have made several changes in SAC planning stories and would like to delete all existing private and public bookmarks as an Admin. While importing from Prod to Q, we do get the option to not bring in Bookmarks but not when exporting from Q to prod.

entry.title: Story 2.0 - bookmark date(BW Variable) was not changing. considering saved date
entry.content: Story 2.0 - bookmark date(BW Variable) was not changing. considering saved date

entry.title: Accumulated Static Actuals in SAC Planning using Data Actions – Step-by-Step Solution
entry.content:
Requirement

In SAP Analytics Cloud Planning, we had a requirement to calculate accumulated (cumulative) actual values over time, but the values should remain static (not recalculated dynamically in stories).

Problem Statement

  • Need cumulative actuals (e.g., Jan → Jan, Feb → Jan+Feb, Mar → Jan+Feb+Mar).
  • Values should be stored in the model (not calculated on the fly).
  • Standard calculated measures were not suitable because they are dynamic.

Solution Approach

Hi Experts,

I would like to share a working solution to calculate Accumulated Actual (Life-To-Date) in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and publish it into the model using Data Action and Multi Action.

🔹 Data Action Script

CONFIG.TIME_HIERARCHY = CALENDARYEAR
CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = OFF

MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = ("Actual", "Life_To_Date")
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "201801" TO PERIOD(TODAY())

VARIABLEMEMBER #prevLTD OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #thisActual OF [d/Measures]

FOREACH [d/Date]

    // Fetch previous month Life-To-Date
    DATA([d/Measures] = #prevLTD) =
        RESULTLOOKUP(
            [d/Measures] = "Life_To_Date",
            [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(1, "MONTH", [d/Date])
        )

    // Fetch current month Actual
    DATA([d/Measures] = #thisActual) =
        RESULTLOOKUP(
            [d/Measures] = "Actual",
            [d/Date] = [d/Date]
        )

    // Calculate cumulative value
    DATA([d/Measures] = "Life_To_Date") =
        RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #prevLTD) +
        RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #thisActual)

    // "Life_To_Date" holds the final output

ENDFOR

entry.title: Variable inheritance issue between classic and optimized stories when opening via links, hyperlinks
entry.content:
Problem Description
In the source story, there is a KPI table with links to other stories.
The behavior when opening these links differs depending on:

The type of target story (classic vs optimized)
Whether the link opens in the same tab or a new tab
The target story configuration (saved or server values)

 

1. Classic Story

When opening the link, the selected variables (Mes/Año and Programa) are correctly inherited in the target story.
The variable prompt appears pre-filled with values from the source story.

2. Optimized Story

If the target story is configured to use server-defined values, it does not inherit the current variables.
If configured to use current variable selections, it works correctly.
Even when adding parameters in the URL (vXXPar, vXXVal or fXXModel, fXXDim, fXXVal), the variable prompt remains empty.

3. Opening Method

Script onClick with NavigationUtils.openStory:
Same tab: sometimes variables are inherited
New tab: variable prompt is empty.
Hyperlink in table or widget:
Same tab: similar behavior to script.
New tab: variable prompt is empty, values not inherited.

entry.title: Cumulative Calculation Across Blended Models in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Hi all,
I am working with three models in SAP Analytics Cloud: Sales, Comp Stock, and Purchase. Each model contains a YearMonth dimension, and I have linked these models in a story using the Link Dimensions (Blending) option. Additionally, Material ID is also used as a linking dimension.

Requirement:
I need to calculate cumulative values (e.g., Quantity, Sales, Cost, Comp Stock) based on the selected YearMonth:

  • If Dec 2025 is selected → cumulative should be from Jan 2025 to Dec 2025
  • If Jan 2026 is selected → cumulative should be from Feb 2025 to Jan 2026 (rolling 12 months)
  • If no YearMonth is selected → it should default to cumulative up to the current month

Issue:
I have tried the following approaches, but none are working correctly due to model blending:

  • Running Total
  • Restricted Measures
  • Aggregation Calculations

These either fail to respect the blended model context or do not compute correctly across models.

Question:
What is the recommended approach to calculate cumulative (especially rolling 12-month) values across blended models in SAC?
Is there a way to handle this via calculated measures, advanced formulas, or model-level changes?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: SAC Chart fails to render when using dimension with text association instead of attribute
entry.content:
In SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) stories using a Datasphere model, a chart fails to render when the dimension "Lieferant" (Supplier) is used directly. The error message displayed is: "No data is available for the chart to render."

 

However, when using the attribute "Lieferant (Business)" of the same dimension — which is simply derived from "Lieferant" and contains only the ID — the chart renders correctly, even with the hierarchy dimension "Bestelldatum" (Order Date).

The dimension "Lieferant" includes a text association for the supplier name. Users need the ability to display both the supplier ID and the supplier text (name) in separate columns within the story. Currently, this is not possible because the chart does not render when the dimension with the text association is used directly.

This behavior is inconsistent: the attribute (ID only) works, but the full dimension (with text association) does not. There is no obvious reason why the dimension itself should fail to render while its attribute works fine.

entry.title: In the context of People Intelligence, can it consume detailed Performance Mgmt. data from SF
entry.content:
In the context of People Intelligence, can it consume and model detailed Performance Management data from SuccessFactors, such as Continuous Feedback and form-based entities (e.g. FormObjective, FormObjectiveSection, FormCompetency, FormCompetencySection), in order to provide analytical reporting on employee-level ratings (not only overall ratings, but also detailed evaluations)?

We are currently facing limitations when accessing these objects via SAC OData connection (e.g. KBA 3490835, sourcetype is missing in request headers, error 400), so the question is whether People Intelligence can natively leverage this data without relying on exports from SF (Integration Center, Story reports)?

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Cannot change Metric chart color
entry.content:
Dear community, 

i have a problem by defining metric. 

I have a column chart which has grey columns in the story and I want to add this chart to a metric list. 

 

 

This works fine, but the chart does not take the colors from the story. It switches to the default blue color and i am not able to change the color in the widget definition. 

I have also tried to change the styling palette in the story theme, but nothings works so far. 

Do you have an idea how i am able to change the color of the widget diagram? 

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Blending Model in ODE not as expected
entry.content:
During the migration of our stories from Classic Mode to the Optimized Design Experience (ODE), we have encountered an issue regarding the behavior of model blending.

Initial Situation (Classic)

In the Classic Mode, it was possible to blend multiple models without requiring the linked dimensions to be part of the table layout (rows/columns).
This allowed for a clean and business-relevant data presentation.

Behavior in ODE

Since using blending in the ODE, we observe the following behavior:

  • Linked dimensions (e.g., Object ID, User ID) must be included in the table drill (rows/columns) for the blending to work correctly.
  • If these dimensions are not included, data is either not displayed or the blending does not behave as expected.

Problem Description

This leads to several limitations:

  • The affected dimensions are not relevant for business display purposes and are intentionally not meant to be shown.
  • Forcing these dimensions into the table results in:
    • unnecessarily complex and overloaded tables
    • reduced readability
    • <

entry.title: refreshData() does not invalidate client-side cache after OData call in Analytics Designer
entry.content:
In an SAP Analytics Cloud Analytic Application built with Analytics Designer, a table widget displays records filtered to status "Not Reviewed". A button triggers an OData service call via ODataService.getEntity() which updates the status of selected records to "Reviewed" in the backend. After the OData call completes successfully, a refreshData() is executed — both at widget level (tbl_Detail.getDataSource().refreshData()) and at application level (Application.refreshData()).

The backend update is confirmed to be successful: when the full page is reloaded and filters are re-applied manually, the processed records no longer appear in the table. However, after refreshData() — whether triggered via script or via the native story refresh button — the records remain visible in the table. Only a complete browser page reload causes the records to disappear.

entry.title: SAC Trace Level - debug?
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I wonder 'Trace Level' in SAC System administration.

I want to see a log more detailed than 'error' trace level which is default option now.

In that case, i have to choice 'debug' level, right?

 

and also that configuration will be reflected in 'job monitor' , correct?

(only fail job, not the success??)

 

There's a some text about this at SAC Help but it is not a detailed

so anyone know about this please share your experience or knowledge please.

thank you!

 

Regards of,

hj.

entry.title: change in Input variables which i am taking from CDS view to show current on load as calendar.
entry.content:
Hello Team 

I had a requirement in SAC dashboard that when dashboard loads it should load on as on date. and user wanted a calendar to pop up to selected the date which was not directly possible in SAC dymanic filtter as it gives look back and front option. so to achive this requirement we used CDS view parameter. Now user want to control this parameter look n feel and error message which it is popluting while selecting the wrong dates. I have tried to change throgh scripting but i am unable to control it. 

Could you Please provide me the solution how should i take it forward.

entry.title: change in Input variables which i am taking from CDS view to show current on load as calendar.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement in an SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) dashboard where, on load, the data should default to the “As On Date” (current date).

Additionally, the user wants a calendar popup to select a date. Since SAC dynamic date filters only provide look back and look forward options, this requirement could not be fulfilled directly using standard filters.

To achieve this, we implemented a CDS view parameter for date selection.

However, we are now facing the following challenges:

  • The look & feel of the CDS parameter prompt (calendar UI) cannot be customized
  • Error messages are displayed when users select invalid dates, and these messages are coming from the backend
  • We are unable to control or override these behaviors using SAC scripting

Questions:

  1. Is there any way in SAC to customize the UI (calendar behavior) of CDS parameters?
  2. Can we override or handle backend error messages within SAC?
  3. What would be the recommended approach to handle such a requirement while ensuring better user experience?

entry.title: SAC Story: How to send notification alert via email when measure in threshold is RED?
entry.content: On SAC Story, How to send notification alert via email when measure in threshold is RED? #SAC

entry.title: SAC Email notifications
entry.content:
We’re looking for a solution that can send an email notification to users when a KPI value drops by a specified amount.

We’ve already tried using the Application.sendNotification() API in SAP Analytics Cloud, but it only generates in-app SAC notifications and does not send emails.

We need a way to ensure that users receive these notifications via email, specifically on a designated day of each month.

entry.title: in sap analytics cloud story how to lookup a value from previous row
entry.content:
Hello Friends

I want to be able to create a shipment report where yesterday's unshipped (Carryover) deliveries show as Opening Balance of the next day.  I can easily do this in BOBJ (see below) using PREVIOUS function.  Since PREVIOUS is not available in SAC, is there a trick to produce similar report?

entry.title: Cascading Filters (story filters) with single selection
entry.content:
Hi all.

SAC ODE Story (BW Live Model).
Requirement: 2 Story filters (Dimension1, Dimension2) must behave as follows:

  • Single Selection.
  • The filter on Dimension1 must affect the filter on Dimension2, but the filter on Dimension2 must NOT affect the first filter.

Explanation: The filter on Dimension1 must always show all members available, regardless of the value selected in Dimension2.

Is it possible?

Thanks in advance,
Luis.
